title: Valve stroke and spindle way counter module for a valve and actuator monitoring system
abstract: The present application provides a method of evaluating spindle component wear in a turbine (100) by a data acquisition system. The method may include the steps of receiving a number of operating parameters from a number of sensors (380), wherein the operating parameters may include spindle position from which a number of spindle strokes and an accumulated spindle distance may be determined and temperature, generating a movement history based on the operating parameters, comparing the movement history to predetermined values, and altering one or more of the operating parameters and/or initiating repair procedures if the movement history exceeds predetermined values.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of evaluating spindle component wear in a turbine (100) by a data acquisition system (710), comprising: receiving a plurality of operating parameters from a plurality of sensors (380); wherein the plurality of operating parameters comprises spindle position from which a number of spindle strokes and an accumulated spindle distance may be determined and temperature; generating a movement history based on the plurality of operating parameters; comparing the movement history to predetermined values; and altering one or more of the plurality of operating parameters and/or initiating repair procedures if the movement history exceeds predetermined values. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the step of altering one or more of the plurality of operating parameters and/or initiating repair procedures comprises repairing and/or replacing a spindle (330, 360), a spindle guide, a spindle seal, and/or a spindle packing. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the step of altering one or more of the plurality of operating parameters and/or initiating repair procedures comprises taking a valve (260) out of service. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the step of altering one or more of the plurality of operating parameters and/or initiating repair procedures comprises ordering parts. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of generating a movement history comprises classifying the spindle strokes according to stroke value. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the step of classifying the spindle strokes according to stroke value comprises classifying the spindle strokes according to size and mean spindle position. 7. The method of claim 5, wherein the step of classifying the spindle strokes according to stroke value comprises filtering small strokes from the stroke value. 8. The method of claim 5, wherein the step of classifying the spindle strokes according to stroke value comprises performing a spindle stroke rainflow analysis. 9. The method of claim 5, wherein the step of classifying the spindle strokes according to stroke value comprises performing a spindle wear analysis, whereby the spindle wear analysis comprises a size of the spindle strokes, a mean value of the spindle strokes, and temperature. 10. A turbine system (100), comprising: a plurality of valves (260); a plurality of sensors (380) capable of receiving turbine and valve operating parameters; and 

 a data acquisition system (710), including a processor (730) in communication with the plurality of sensors (380) and wherein the data acquisition system (710) is operable to perform the following operations: receiving the plurality of turbine and valve operating parameters from the plurality of sensors (380); wherein the plurality of turbine and valve operating parameters comprises spindle position from which a number of spindle strokes and an accumulated spindle distance may be determined and temperature; generating a movement history based on the plurality of operating parameters; comparing the movement history to predetermined values; and estimating time until required valve repair. 1 1. The turbine system (100) of claim 10, wherein the data acquisition system (710) is further operable to alter one or more of the plurality of operating parameters and/or to take a valve (260) out of service if the movement history exceeds a predetermined value. 12. The turbine system (100) of claim 10, wherein the data acquisition system (710) is further operable to initiate repair procedures if the movement history exceeds a predetermined value. 13. The turbine system (100) of claim 10, wherein the data acquisition system (710) is further operable to order parts if the movement history exceeds a predetermined value. 14. The turbine system (100) of claim 10, wherein the plurality of valves (260) comprises one or more steam stop valves (270) and one or more steam control valves (280) 15. The turbine system (100) of claim 10, wherein the plurality of valves (260) comprises a spindle (330, 360), a spindle guide, a spindle seal, and a spindle packing.
